name: schedule
description: Manage your calendar — view events and create new ones

tools.name: list_events
tools.description: Lists upcoming calendar events with title, time, attendees, and location. Use when the user asks 'what's on my calendar', 'what meetings do I have', 'show my schedule', or 'what's next'. Returns events for the specified time window (default: today). Do NOT call again if calendar events were already retrieved — refer to previous results for follow-up questions about times, conflicts, or attendees.

days_ahead.description: How many days ahead to look for events. Default is 1 (today only).

tools.name: add_event
tools.description: Creates a new event on the user's calendar with a title and time. Use when the user asks to 'schedule a meeting', 'add to my calendar', 'set a reminder', 'book a time', or 'create a calendar block'. This is a WRITE action — use it to complete chained tasks like 'find X in my email and add it to my calendar'. When chaining from another tool's output, extract the relevant details (date, title, amount) and pass them directly — do NOT ask the user to confirm details you already have. Always provide both title and time.

title.description: Title of the calendar event. When chaining from email, use the subject or relevant detail (e.g. 'Review AWS bill - $847.32').

time.description: Date and time of the event. Examples: 'today at noon', '2026-04-04 09:00', 'Friday at 3pm'.
